title: Leak-safe empirical hazard priors
summary: The current best solution hand-codes tyre life windows.
rationale: The current best solution hand-codes tyre life windows. Public F1 strategy research treats pit timing as a lap-level decision driven by tyre degradation and race context, so smoothed empirical next-lap hazard tables can learn circuit-, stint-, compound-, and race-progress-specific stopping windows directly.
implementation_hint: Generate out-of-fold Bayesian target encodings for bins such as Race x Compound x Stint x TyreLifeBin, Race x LapsRemainingBin, Year x Compound x Stint, and WetRegime x RaceProgressBin. Use strong smoothing toward global and group parents; fit encoders only on training folds and map test using full-train encoders.
expected_effect: Can capture non-obvious circuit/stint pit timing patterns beyond fixed soft/medium/hard expected-life constants, improving AUC if CV is race-aware.
risk: High leakage risk if encodings are not OOF. With only 26 races, fine-grained tables can overfit; smoothing and fallback hierarchy are essential.
